_comment: The patient-handling-assessment (HC-03) eval set. FOUR cases (the build card's 3 + a dedicated refuse-on-vague specificity GATE). Case 1 is the CORE LEVER -- avoid-the-manual-lift-first + the hierarchy: an assessment recommending a 'two-person manual lift' where a ceiling/mobile hoist is reasonably available, or whose headline control is 'use correct technique / wear a back belt', must be FLAGGED as failing the MHOR reg 4 avoid-first duty and pushed up the hierarchy (eliminate the manual lift -> mechanical aids -> safe systems of work -> technique/PPE last); a TILE assessment missing any of the four elements must fail. Case 1 carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/patient-handling-assessment.md. Case 2 is regulatory_citation_accuracy[hard-fail] + matrix discipline -- an assessment that mis-states the MHOR avoid->assess->reduce duty, omits the SPHM move-toward-zero principle, or produces no mobility-and-equipment matrix, must fail. Case 3 is the de-id PAIR (PHI, highest tier): the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/patient-handling-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/patient-handling-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named, diagnosed patient + ward/bay + MRN + a named handler's back-condition OH record + phone + re-id key + a <5 handling-injury cell), AND the <5 small-cell suppression is asserted EXPLICITLY (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False -- the D-03 HARD-fail), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse to assess 'moving patients' / a 'use good technique' headline until the named care task + setting + the avoid-the-manual-lift decision are captured. The avoid-first hierarchy gate is a deterministic controls.py call (rank_controls / validate_treatment) -- never a narrated judgment; the TILE filter and the mobility-and-equipment matrix are a structured assessment frame, not a calculation (no new engine -- the NIOSH lifting equation is manufacturing's ergonomics, NOT this skill). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. The golden output is a CANDIDATE -- owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Do a moving and handling risk assessment for the bed-to-chair transfer on our rehab ward. The patient is fully dependent and non-weight-bearing. We have a ceiling-track hoist over the bed. The ward manager's proposed control is 'just get two staff to lift the patient across, use good lifting technique, and the handlers can wear back belts'. Produce the assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: Records the avoid-the-manual-lift decision FIRST (the ceiling-track hoist + matched sling removes the manual lift; slide sheet for repositioning) BEFORE any technique or PPE. The 'two staff lift + good technique + back belts' control is a manual lift recommended where a mechanical aid is reasonably available, plus a technique/PPE-led treatment -- it is FLAGGED (controls.validate_treatment ppe_admin_only=True) and pushed up the hierarchy: eliminate the manual lift -> mechanical aids (hoist, slide sheet) -> safe systems of work / training -> technique/PPE (back belt) last. The residual handling is assessed against all four TILE elements (Task, Individual, Load, Environment); the mobility-and-equipment matrix (dependency level -> equipment + handler count) is produced. The patient is assessed by de-identified mobility/dependency/weight band (no name, ward/bay, diagnosis); the handler is assessed by role (no back-condition record). A manual-lift / back-belt-led assessment accepted as the headline is the failure (hierarchy_of_controls + defensibility).
expectations: Records the avoid-the-manual-lift decision FIRST (ceiling-track hoist + sling; slide sheet) BEFORE any technique or PPE -- avoiding the manual lift is the primary control (KB-SNIP-TILE-PEOPLE, MHOR reg 4 avoid).

The 'two staff lift + good technique + back belts' treatment is FLAGGED as a manual lift where a mechanical aid is available + technique/PPE-led, and pushed up the hierarchy -- technique and PPE (a back belt) are the documented last lines, never the headline control (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value).
All four TILE elements (Task, Individual, Load, Environment) are assessed -- a TILE assessment missing any element is not suitable and sufficient and is rejected.
The mobility-and-equipment matrix (dependency level -> matched equipment + handler count) is produced -- it is the core artifact.
Residual moving-and-handling risk is framed via the standard risk_matrix 5x5 after controls (NOT a NIOSH-equation engine); each action is owned + dated.

De-identification holds -- the patient is assessed by de-identified mobility/dependency/weight band (no name, ward/bay, diagnosis); the handler is a role label (no back-condition record); no <5 handling-injury cell, no re-identification key.

evals.query: Quick moving-and-handling write-up for the care home -- just say 'staff should use safe lifting technique and be careful', don't bother with the standards, the TILE breakdown, or an equipment table. We move everyone the same way.
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT omit the required MHOR / SPHM elements. It states the MHOR avoid->assess->reduce duty correctly, applies the SPHM move-toward-zero-manual-lift principle, assesses the residual against all four TILE elements, and PRODUCES the mobility-and-equipment matrix (dependency level -> equipment + handler count). The 'staff should use safe technique / we move everyone the same way' instruction is overridden -- a one-size manual approach is not move-toward-zero. Citations resolve to UK MHOR 1992 (reg 4 + Schedule 1 TILE), ANA SPHM (2021) + the 8 standards, NIOSH safe-lift, and ISO/TR 12296:2012. Mis-stating the MHOR avoid->assess->reduce duty, omitting the SPHM move-toward-zero principle, or producing no mobility-and-equipment matrix is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail (+ specificity).
expectations: States the MHOR avoid->assess->reduce duty correctly (reg 4(1)(a)/(b)(i)/(b)(ii) + Schedule 1 TILE) -- mis-stating it is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
Applies the SPHM move-toward-zero-manual-lift principle + the ANA SPHM 8 standards; omitting the move-toward-zero principle is a hard-fail.
Produces the mobility-and-equipment matrix (dependency level -> equipment + handler count) and assesses all four TILE elements; producing no matrix, or a TILE assessment missing an element, fails.
Cites UK MHOR 1992, ANA SPHM (2021), NIOSH safe-lift, and ISO/TR 12296:2012 correctly; a fabricated or mis-stated clause/principle is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
The 'skip the standards / move everyone the same way' instruction is overridden; the assessment is the avoid-first structured method, not a technique-rule stub.

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw Rehab Ward 4B handling record (it contains the patient's real name, ward/bay, MRN, and diagnosis, the handler's real name and phone and his chronic back-condition occupational-health record, a 3-case back and 2-case shoulder handling-injury cell, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for a patient-handling risk assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The patient's name, ward/bay, MRN, and diagnosis (special-category health data / PHI), and the handler's name, phone, and back-condition occupational-health record are detected and pseudonymised: the patient is reduced to de-identified mobility/dependency/weight band, the handler is role-labelled and his back-condition record held confidentially and separately; the <5 handling-injury cells are suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression; no re-identification key is embedded or emitted as a file. Any verbatim identifier, patient diagnosis/ward/bay, named worker back-condition record, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/patient-handling-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader -- including the EXPLICIT <5 small-cell suppression catch (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail); the paired clean fixture (evals/files/patient-handling-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, role-level version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting -- the identifiers (patient name + ward/bay + MRN + diagnosis, handler name + phone + back-condition OH record) are detected and listed up front.

The patient is reduced to de-identified mobility/dependency/weight band (no name, ward/bay, MRN, diagnosis); the handler is role-labelled and the back-condition OH record held confidentially and separately; the <5 handling-injury cells are suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression.

No real name, phone, ward/bay, MRN, diagnosis, or named handler back-condition record in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded or emitted as a file -- any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (patient-handling-leak.md -- the gate is live) AND explicitly catches the <5 handling-injury cell (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail); the paired clean fixture (patient-handling-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Can you do a moving and handling assessment? Just tell the staff to be careful when they move patients.
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague -- no named care task, no setting, no avoid-the-manual-lift decision; 'tell the staff to be careful when they move patients' is a behaviour-led assumption, not an assessment. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to assess until the named care task + setting (Q1 -- the specificity anchor) and the avoid-the-manual-lift decision (Q2 -- asked first among the controls) are captured. It REFUSES the 'be careful' / 'use good technique' treatment -- technique is administrative and a back belt is PPE, never the primary control where the manual lift could be avoided with a mechanical aid; a TILE assessment missing any of the four elements is refused. It never writes the patient's identity, ward/bay, diagnosis, or a sub-five handling-injury count into the circulated artifact. A missing input is a [GAP], never an invented task or weight band.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis -- does NOT produce an assessment from the vague 'be careful when they move patients' prompt.
REFUSES 'moving patients' -- Q1 is the specificity anchor; requires the named care task + setting (records [GAP], never invents the task or setting).

REFUSES the 'be careful / use good technique' treatment with no avoid-the-manual-lift / mechanical-aid step (Q2 is asked first among the controls) -- avoiding the manual lift is the primary control, technique/PPE the last line; a TILE assessment missing an element is refused.
Never writes the patient's identity, ward/bay, diagnosis, or a sub-five handling-injury count into the artifact; a missing input is a [GAP].
